category_title: Chamorro 5th-grade student with ADHD and literacy challenges
minimal_context: Juan is participating in a small group oral retelling session of the tale 'The Old Lady in the Vinegar Bottle'.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for our small group oral retelling of 'The Old Lady in the Vinegar Bottle'.

STUDENT: Okay whose turn?

TUTOR: It is your turn. Please start from the beginning.

STUDENT: Okay. It feels like magic, for example it just like all comes out. The story that I told and heard. A magic tape recorder. (Starts from the beginning) Once there was an old lady...

TUTOR: Keep going.

STUDENT: (Stumbles when he can't recall some words) I can't remember the next part. It’s fun, because everyone’s in a group. We’re all forgetting, but we’re all helping each other. Someone tell me the word.

options.A: Reduce the overall size of the oral retelling task so the student can successfully complete it.
options.B: Provide a collaborative small group setting to practice verbal discourse and recall of the tale.
options.C: Explain the tale, words, or expressions using drawing, translation, or body language during the session.
options.D: Allow the student to select a positive reinforcer before the retelling, earning it upon completion or an honest attempt.
